location_precision.why: Every finding carries a usable file:line (e.g. "src/sync.py:14", "src/report.py:11").

interaction_synthesis.why: Findings are listed independently with no cross-defect mechanism stated—e.g. the error-swallowing at :39 and the mark-seen-before-update at :31 are never connected into a compound failure mode.

causal_clarity.why: Essentially every finding states the failure mechanism, e.g. :24 explains "Mutable default argument seen=[] is shared across all calls, so any user id synced once is permanently skipped."

location_precision.why: Every one of the seven findings carries a usable file:line, e.g. "src/sync.py:31" and "src/report.py:11".

interaction_synthesis.why: Each finding stands alone with no cross-references, and no compound failure mode (e.g. the swallowed exception at :39 combining with the premature seen-marking at :31) is ever articulated.

causal_clarity.why: Every finding states a mechanism, e.g. ":7 ... early `return 0` ... never reaches `f.close()`, leaking the file handle" and ":35 f-string interpolation ... introducing SQL injection and breaking on emails containing a quote."

location_precision.why: Every finding carries a usable file:line (e.g. "src/sync.py:14", "src/report.py:11").

interaction_synthesis.why: Each defect is listed in isolation with no explanation of how any two combine into a compound failure mode (e.g. the swallowed errors at line 39 are never linked to the premature seen-marking at line 31).

causal_clarity.why: Every finding states a failure mechanism, e.g. "appended to `seen` before the UPDATE runs, so a record whose sync fails is marked as seen and never retried."
